creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
sheldonh@FreeBSD.org once wrote:

> Synopsis: math/siag port fixed, upgraded to 3.1.16
 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->closed
> State-Changed-By: sheldonh
> State-Changed-When: Mon Jun 7 11:45:31 PDT 1999
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Please resubmit diff(1) output as per the instructions at
> http://www.freebsd.org/handbook/porting.html .

This  sort  of response  may  be  good  for  training your  children  or
servants:  "Please,  get   out,  close  the  door,  and   come  back  in
as  a  well  behaving  young  gentleman".  I take  this  is  a  hint,  I
need  FreeBSD  more  then  FreeBSD  needs me  (as  a  porter).  No,  I'm
not  going  to  resubmit  it  this  way.  As  I  wrote  in  my  PR  (see
http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=12059):

	My version of  port is quite different from the  old one, so
	please  forgive me  for providing  the complete  replacement
	rather patches.

This would indicate to  a anyone that I submitted the way  I did NOT out
of ignorance (which  _may_ be deserving of this kind  of treatment), but
because  I  felt that  to  be  the right  thing  to  do. Anyone  feeling
otherwise can run the diff(1) against his/her own /usr/ports/math/siag.

At this point, anyone can get  the uuencoded tarball from the URL above,
make the diffs and re-submit them. But it will not be me.

	-mi
